resources.string: Tabletop mode. Place your device with the charging port to the left.
To update dot position, hold down %d fingers.
There is no other keyboard enabled – go to keyboard settings
Swipe up with three fingers for more options
TalkBack braille keyboard – for help, visit g.co/help/braille
Updating dot position. First, let’s record the dots on your %s.
Touch and hold the screen with the middle three fingers of your %s.
Touch and hold the screen with the last four fingers of your %s.
Updated positions for dots 1, 2 and 3. Next, let’s record the dots on your %s.
Updated positions for dots 1, 2, 3 and 7. Next, let’s record the dots on your %s.
Touch and hold the screen with %d fingers. Then try again.
To use the braille keyboard, open your regular keyboard, then tap the choose keyboard icon %1$s (usually in the bottom-right corner). Select \'%2$s\'.

1. Tap 'Settings' below. On the next screen, turn on '%1$s'

2. Open your regular keyboard, then tap switch input method %2$s (usually in the bottom-right corner).

3. Select '%1$s'.
If you don\'t see an option to switch keyboards, try installing %s, the Google Keyboard.

1. Turn on braille keyboard below.

2. Open your regular keyboard, then tap switch input method %2$s (usually in the bottom-right corner).

3. Select '%1$s'.
If you can\'t switch keyboards, try installing %s, the Google Keyboard.
To change your language while typing with the braille keyboard, swipe up with three fingers. Then, select \'switch to next language\'.
Each pressed dot is part of the input, even if the dot is released before other dots
Swap the dot positions, so that dots one and four trade places, for example
Hold %1$s, then swipe %2$s anywhere with another finger
Hold %1$s, then swipe %2$s anywhere with %3$s fingers
Add spaces, delete, hide the keyboard and more
You can switch granularity to have your cursor move by character, word or line. Or control how your cursor moves with a series of gestures that include braille dots.
Move by character, word or line
Check the spelling and grammar within a text box
You can use proofreading to check for spelling and grammar suggestions. When you\'re typing or editing text, use three fingers to swipe left or right until you hear \'Proofread\'.
Your device doesn’t support braille keyboard
Your device doesn’t support enough touch points. For more information, contact your device manufacturer.
Turn on TalkBack to use braille keyboard
Resume TalkBack to use braille keyboard
When TalkBack is on, you can tap your screen to enter braille symbols.
Turn on TalkBack to change gesture
TalkBack is off. Turn it on to change braille keyboard gestures.
All braille keyboard gestures will be reset to default
The gesture will be reset to \'%s\'.\n\nIf any other action is using this gesture, it will be replaced.
This action uses the same gesture as \'%s\'. Changing this gesture will change it for both actions.

On the next screen, braille keyboard will open and you won't be able to use TalkBack gestures.

When the braille keyboard opens, perform the gesture that you want to use, or, to close braille keyboard and cancel the gesture change, swipe down with 2 fingers.

See available gestures
Do you also want to use the \'%1$s\' action with the mirrored gesture (%2$s)?
Do you also want to use the mirrored gesture (%1$s) for the \'%2$s\' action?\n\nThis will replace \'%3$s\' for the mirrored gesture.

With the braille keyboard, you can tap your screen to enter 6-dot braille.

You’re using Unified English Braille. To switch your typing language, go to TalkBack settings.

To type braille, you need to hold and use your phone differently.
You won’t be able to use regular TalkBack gestures in the braille keyboard.\n\nTo get started, let’s set up the position of the six braille dots when you type.
Starting now, you won’t be able to use regular TalkBack gestures. TalkBack isn’t available while using the braille keyboard.
To start, hold your phone like this, with the screen facing away from you.
Tap dot one with your left index finger to type the letter \'A\'
Next, try typing the letters B, C and D
To delete a letter, swipe left
To add a space, swipe right
To move the cursor backwards, swipe up
To move the cursor forwards, swipe down
To hide keyboard, swipe down with two fingers
To switch to your other keyboard, swipe down with three fingers
To finish tutorial, swipe up with three fingers
Starting now, you won’t be able to use regular TalkBack gestures. TalkBack isn’t available while using the braille keyboard.\n\n%1$s

First, turn your phone so that the screen is facing away from you.

Next, rotate the phone horizontally to landscape position. Your hands should grip the short ends of the phone. Your right hand should hold the end with the USB port. Rest your three middle fingers on the screen, and use your little finger and thumb to support the phone.

When you’re ready, double tap the screen to continue.

To exit braille keyboard, swipe down toward the ground with three fingers. This switches back to your original keyboard.

Your phone is in a vertical position. To continue, rotate your phone horizontally so that the long side is on the bottom. Then turn the screen to face away from you. When you’re ready, double tap the screen to continue.

Keep the screen faced away from you. Hold your phone in both hands, on the short edges of the phone, so that the middle three fingers of each hand can touch the screen. Your left index finger should be on dot one. Now, tap dot one to type the letter \'A\'.
Tap dot one with your left index finger to type \'A\'
To delete a letter, swipe left. Try it now.
To add a space, swipe right. Try it now.
To move the cursor backwards, swipe up. Try it now.
To move the cursor forwards, swipe down. Try it now.
To hide the keyboard, swipe down with two fingers. Try it now.
Hide keyboard. Usually, this will hide the keyboard. The braille keyboard will stay open because you’re still in the tutorial.
To switch to your other keyboard, swipe down with three fingers. Try it now.
Next keyboard. Usually, this will switch to the next keyboard. This time, you’re staying in the braille keyboard because you’re still in the tutorial.
You can also type in tabletop mode by placing your phone flat on a table. To finish the tutorial, swipe up with three fingers, and find the finish tutorial option
The options menu is open. Flip your phone back so that it’s facing you and hold it normally. You can now use regular TalkBack gestures to navigate. Swipe right to see more options.
%s\nPlace your device flat on a table. Then, touch and hold the screen with the middle three fingers of each hand in a position that’s comfortable for you to type.
Place your device flat on a table. Then, touch and hold the screen with the middle three fingers of each hand in a position that’s comfortable for you to type.
Showing braille keyboard. Turn your phone so that the screen is facing away from you. Rotate the phone horizontally to landscape position. Then start typing.
An external braille display is connected, which you can use to type text into your phone.\n\nYou can also switch to the touchscreen braille keyboard or the next input method.
You can use the connected external braille display to enter text on this device.\n\nYou can also switch to the next input method.
Perform the gesture that you want to use for %s
To cancel, swipe down with 2 fingers
To confirm, perform this gesture again.
This gesture is being used for \'%1$s\'. To replace it, perform this gesture again. %2$s
This gesture is used for \'%1$s\' and can\'t be changed. Try another gesture. %2$s
Try another gesture. To see the list of all available gestures, go to the TalkBack braille keyboard Help Centre. %s
Perform the gesture that you want to use again. %s
